title: Matar Pulao (Peas Pulao)

ingredients.name: basmati rice, soaked 20 minutes and drained

subs.name: regular long-grain rice, add 2 tbsp extra water

steps.text: Heat oil in a saucepan or small pot over medium flame. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon and cardamom. Fry for 30 seconds until the spices are fragrant.
Add sliced onion and cook, stirring often, until pale golden — about 5 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until the raw smell is gone.
Add the green peas and stir for 30 seconds to coat them in the spiced oil. If using frozen peas there is no need to thaw first.
Add the soaked, drained basmati rice and gently stir for 1 minute to toast the grains. Season with salt and pour in the water. Bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce to the lowest possible flame, cover tightly and cook for 12 minutes without lifting the lid.
Remove from heat and rest covered for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork, picking out the whole spices. Garnish with coriander and serve immediately with dal tadka, curd or raita.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: peas → corn kernels or diced carrots; basmati-rice → regular long-grain rice, add 2 tbsp extra water
